candidates.name: YouTube audio extraction pipeline
candidates.detail: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extraction to MP3. Full lifecycle in ~30 lines. Reference for T27 YouTube content extraction.

candidates.name: Amazon Transcribe async job lifecycle
candidates.detail: Start transcription job -> poll -> fetch transcript from S3 -> cleanup. Shows speaker labels, language config, S3 output routing. Reference for T27 speech-to-text options.

candidates.name: PII tokenize/untokenize round-trip pattern
candidates.detail: Comprehend PII detection -> token replacement (T1/T2/T3) -> token map persistence -> LLM processing with tokens -> untokenization. Preserves context while anonymizing. Prompt template instructs model to use tokens as names.

candidates.name: Chain of Responsibility + Factory handler composition
candidates.detail: Reader/processor/writer handler taxonomy with set_next() chaining. Factory with lazy import and caching. Clean pipeline composition model.

candidates.name: AST-based handler auto-discovery
candidates.detail: HandlerFactory.discover_handlers walks handler tree, parses Python ASTs to find AbstractHandler subclasses, registers for lazy import. Zero-config extension model.

candidates.name: JSONPath model-agnostic invocation config
candidates.detail: Environment-driven model request/response mapping via JSONPath expressions. Same code invokes Claude v2, Claude 3, or Titan by changing env vars.

candidates.name: Amazon Transcribe API reference
candidates.detail: Core speech-to-text service API. Handler demonstrates full async lifecycle with speaker diarization.

candidates.name: pytubefix YouTube download + caption library
candidates.detail: 1.5K stars, MIT, actively maintained (Mar 2026). Key T27 capabilities: (1) Built-in caption/subtitle extraction to SRT — may skip Whisper/Transcribe for captioned videos, (2) Audio-only stream download, (3) Playlist + Channel enumeration, (4) Search API with filters, (5) AsyncYouTube for non-blocking agent use, (6) OAuth for age-restricted content.

candidates.name: AWS-coupled extraction anti-pattern
candidates.detail: 5+ AWS service dependencies (Transcribe, Textract, Comprehend, Bedrock, S3, Rekognition) create vendor lock-in, billing complexity, and failure surface. Prefer local-first alternatives (Whisper, Tesseract, spaCy) for portable tools. The repo itself has a commented-out Whisper handler hinting at this direction.

candidates.name: Blocking poll anti-pattern
candidates.detail: Transcribe handler polls with time.sleep(30) in a blocking while loop. Acceptable for CLI, but must not be copied into agent pipelines. Use async callbacks or event-driven patterns instead.

cross_repo_connections.detail: AWS-MEF is the enterprise frame extraction architecture. This repo is the practical multi-format extraction toolkit. Together they cover T27 from both the video-specific (frames, scenes, shots) and general-content (YouTube, PDF, web, email) angles.
Acknowledged in README as inspiration source. General Bedrock getting-started, not analyzed.
Core dependency for YouTube download. Active YouTube extraction library. Not analyzed yet — potential future repo-analysis target for T27.
